overview.description: Czech software company delivering HR/payroll enterprise software and AI-driven digital transformation services
overview.description_long: OKsystem is a Prague-based family-owned software company operating since 1990, with over 500 employees and offices in Prague, Brno, and Ostrava. The company builds and maintains enterprise products including OKbase (HR/HCM/payroll), Checkbot (Industry 4.0 quality control), Scormium (eLearning), and OKadresy (address validation). Its internal OKlab unit researches and implements AI, robotics, data analytics and cybersecurity. Core clients include Czech public administration (Ministry of Labour and Social Affairs), multinational manufacturers, and large retail chains. Listed by Forbes Czech as one of the top 101 largest family businesses in the country.
overview.tagline: Your first choice for development and innovation

history.origin_story: Founded in Prague in 1990, OKsystem grew from a domestic software provider into one of the Czech Republic's largest family-owned IT companies, specialising in HR/payroll systems and enterprise digital transformation. The company has served some clients for more than 30 years.

key_milestones.event: Began supplying payroll system to Czech Ministry of Foreign Affairs
Started long-term cooperation with Eberspächer on payroll/time-attendance
Expanded OKbase to cover 7,000+ city and regional authority employees
Acquired majority stake in Sense4code (Scormium eLearning platform)
Named IT partner of the Czech Tennis Federation; ranked 101st in Forbes Czech largest family businesses

leadership.name: Martin Procházka
leadership.role: Chairman of the Board of Directors

leadership.name: Vítězslav Ciml
leadership.role: CEO & Vice Chairman of the Board

awards: Forbes Czech Top 101 Largest Family Businesses (2026)
ProcessUnity Global Risk Exchange trusted IT vendor
notable_clients: Ministry of Labour and Social Affairs (Czech Republic)

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: OKsystem's core OKbase HR/payroll platform has deep enterprise integrations and 30+ years of customer lock-in, reducing short-term displacement risk. However, AI-native HR tools (Workday AI, SAP SuccessFactors) are increasingly automating payroll and HCM workflows, making the core product vulnerable over the medium term. OKlab is exploring AI but it is not yet a revenue-generating differentiator.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: The company has a diversified base of long-tenure enterprise and public-sector clients in a stable Czech HR/payroll market. Multiple government ministry contracts provide recurring revenue, and the company has been profitable and growing for 36 years.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: OKsystem is a bootstrapped family-owned company with 500+ employees, 36 years of operations, and substantial recurring government and enterprise contracts. No external funding dependence; ranked among the largest Czech family businesses by Forbes.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Strong customer lock-in through deep enterprise integrations, long-standing government contracts, and 36-year track record make OKsystem resilient. Medium AGI displacement risk on core HR/payroll products is offset by low market and funding risk.

metadata.notes: No CNAIP URL available. Website fully accessible. Bootstrapped family company — no external funding rounds. OKlab is the AI innovation unit. Employees listed as '500+' on About page and '500+ zaměstnanců' on careers page; mapped to 501-1000 bucket.
